deeperExplanation: Python uses EAFP ('Easier to Ask for Forgiveness than Permission') style. Instead of pre-checking `if file_exists()`, pythonic code tries `open()` and catches `FileNotFoundError`.
internalImplementation: CPython manages exception handlers using `SETUP_FINALLY` and `POP_EXCEPT` bytecode instructions.

performanceConsiderations.timeComplexity: O(1) setup cost when no exception occurs.
